summary: Tests are solid for the happy path and optional fields, but lack coverage for orphan features (features referencing non-existent epics are silently included unlike computeState which drops them), multi-epic interleaving, and empty-string assignee edge case. Implementation is clean and minimal but uses mutable accumulation inconsistent with immutable-first conventions.

suggestions.description: No test for orphan features (features referencing non-existent epics)
suggestions.rationale: computeState explicitly drops orphan features (line 130-144 of tests), but toProjectEntities includes them with a parentId pointing to nothing. This is either an inconsistency bug or an intentional design difference, but no test documents the expected behavior either way. A consumer building a tree from these entities would have a dangling parentId.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test with a feature whose epicId does not appear in parsed.epics. Assert whether it should be included or excluded, then align the implementation with that decision.

suggestions.description: Empty-string assignee treated as absent due to truthiness check
suggestions.rationale: The spread pattern `...(feature.frontmatter.assignee ? {...} : {})` uses JavaScript truthiness. An empty string assignee would be silently omitted. If the upstream type allows empty strings, this is a data-loss bug.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test with assignee set to empty string. If it should be omitted, the test documents that. If it should be preserved, change the check to `\!== undefined`.

suggestions.description: No test for in_progress task status
suggestions.rationale: Tests cover completed and not_started task statuses but never exercise in_progress. While the mapping is a simple passthrough, all three valid TaskStatus values should appear in tests to prevent regressions if status mapping logic is ever added.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a task with status in_progress to one of the existing tests, or add a dedicated test for it.

suggestions.description: Mutable array accumulation with push() instead of functional flatMap
suggestions.rationale: The function declares a mutable array and pushes into it imperatively, then returns it as readonly. A flatMap approach would be more consistent with the immutable-first conventions and would eliminate the mutable intermediate state.
suggestions.suggestion: Refactor to use Array.flatMap or array spread: e.g. return [...parsed.epics.flatMap(epicToEntities), ...parsed.features.flatMap(featureToEntities)].

suggestions.description: Ordering test only covers single-epic single-feature scenario
suggestions.rationale: The ordering test proves epics come before features come before tasks with exactly one of each. It does not prove the invariant holds with multiple epics and multiple features, where interleaving could occur.
suggestions.suggestion: Add a test with 2 epics and 2 features (one per epic) each with tasks, and assert that all epics come first, then all features, then all tasks. This would reveal if the current loop-nesting produces epic1, feature1, tasks1, epic2, feature2, tasks2 instead.
